mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters. It simplifies the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential complexity in managing RabbitMQ configurations.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repo
steps.description:
This step adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

helm repo update

```

> This is the official Helm repo for the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages the deployment and lifecycle of RabbitMQ clusters.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.0 --namespace rabbitmq --create-namespace

```

> Installs the operator in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined YAML configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> Lists all pods in the 'rabbitmq' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if the RabbitMQ cluster is not deleted properly.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq

```

> Uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Deletes the 'rabbitmq' namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your RabbitMQ data. This step will upgrade the operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.0 --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> Drains the RabbitMQ pods before upgrading.
> Upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Verifies that the RabbitMQ pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to network issues or incorrect Helm repository configuration.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' and ensure your network connection is stable.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the cluster to schedule the RabbitMQ pods.

**Fix:**
Check your cluster resource limits and consider scaling your nodes.

troubleshooting.title: RabbitMQ cluster is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to incorrect service configuration or network policies.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure network policies allow traffic.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on RabbitMQ pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate a misconfiguration in the RabbitMQ settings.

**Fix:**
Check the RabbitMQ logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved handling of cluster scaling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
